metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: US Stocks Waver as Oil Advances Amid Rising Yields: Markets Wrap
all.description: US stocks struggled for direction as oil climbed amid mounting disruptions to energy markets from the war in the Middle East. The yield on 10-year Treasuries advanced for a fourth straight day.

all.headline: Treasury Yields Reach Three-Week High as Inflation Fears Grow
all.description: Treasuries fell for a fourth day as rising crude oil prices due to the Iran war added to concern that inflation will accelerate.

all.headline: Dettol-Maker Reckitt Keeps Outlook on Emerging Market Growth
all.description: Reckitt Benckiser Group Plc said its revenue growth targets for 2026 remain on track, with strong demand for its cleaning and health products in markets like China and India offsetting a slowdown in E

all.headline: Wall Street Sees AI’s ‘Creative Destruction’ Coming For Entire Companies
all.description: A new worry is rippling across the stock market lately: entire businesses, not just their employees, may be thrown out of work. While most economists say fears of an AI job apocalypse are overblown, s

all.headline: Loan for DuPont Unit Buy Prices at Second-Biggest 2026 Discount
all.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. priced a downsized $1.1 billion acquisition-tied leveraged loan at the second-biggest discount of the year, after initially cool investor response to chemical maker Arclin Inc

all.headline: The Euro and the Loonie Tell You Where Investors See Risk
all.description: Common currency slumps on oil-price surge that lifts the Canadian dollar.

all.headline: SRT Sales Hit Record Pace as Banks Expand Use to Hedge Risks
all.description: Banks are issuing significant risk transfers at a record pace as they use the tool to hedge a wider range of loans, including leveraged buyouts and fund financing.

all.headline: Rehn Says ECB Must Maintain 'Cool Head' Amid Iran War
all.description: The European Central Bank should avoid knee-jerk reactions to the war in Iran and stick to its data-focused approach to setting interest rates, according to Governing Council member Olli Rehn. "My adv

all.headline: High-Grade Firms Snap Week-Long Absence from Europe Debt Market
all.description: Borrowers including Baker Hughes Co. are offering Europe’s first high-grade corporate debt deals in a week, as activity cranks up following several days of near standstill due to conflict in the Middl

all.headline: Asia’s Deepening Oil Crunch Triggers Fuel Hoarding, Price Hikes
all.description: A deepening energy crunch across Asia is impacting all corners of the oil market, with suppliers of everything from shipping fuel to cooking gas beginning to cut back on sales in order to manage shrin

all.headline: South East Water faces £22m fine for supply failures
all.description: The firm was unable to cope during high demand, Ofwat says, leading to "immense stress" for customers.

all.headline: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
all.description: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

all.headline: Are UK interest rates still expected to fall soon?
all.description: The interest rate set by the Bank of England affects mortgage, loan and savings rates for millions.

all.headline: Will petrol and diesel prices go up now?
all.description: If oil prices remain high for some time, the knock-on effects could affect the costs of fuel and food

all.headline: Stock markets and oil prices still volatile over fears Iran war may drag on
all.description: Experts have warned that if oil and gas prices remains elevated it could make goods and services more expensive.

all.headline: We have more privacy controls yet less privacy than ever
all.description: Has online privacy become  "a luxury not a right" for us all in 2026?

all.headline: Musk tells jury 'people read too much' into his posts
Higher tariffs likely this week, says US Treasury
all.description: Scott Bessent says that "likely sometime this week" the US will increase its global tariff on imports from the existing 10%.

all.headline: Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral
all.description: The case is the first wrongful death case against Google over alleged harms caused by Gemini.

all.headline: Remote work, offices shut: Tech giants scramble to respond as Iran war escalates
all.description: The region has been positioning itself as an AI hub, following billions of dollars in investment by tech giants.

all.headline: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
all.description: The Iranian military's strikes come amid speculation that the US wants Iranian Kurdish groups to join its conflict with Iran.

all.headline: Pentagon identifies six US soldiers killed in Iran conflict
all.description: The US soldiers were killed after a drone evaded air defences to hit a command centre in Port Shuaiba, Kuwait, on Sunday.

all.headline: US sub sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Hegseth says
all.description: Sri Lanka's navy says around 140 people are feared missing after a military vessel went down off its southern coast.

all.headline: Trade court orders tariff refunds in setback for Trump administration
all.description: A trade court has cleared the way for businesses to receive refunds for tariffs that the Supreme Court struck down last month.

all.headline: Millions of Cubans plunged into darkness as fuel crisis deepens
all.description: Fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US oil blockade since the seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

all.headline: Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones as tanker sinks in Mediterranean
all.description: The Arctic Metagaz went down between Libya and Malta after it was hit by explosions and a fire, Libyan officials say.

all.headline: Spain's Pedro Sánchez hits back at Trump threat to sever trade saying 'no to war'
all.description: Spain's prime minister delivers a strong rebuttal to US President Donald Trump's threat to end trade with his country.

all.headline: South Africa's long-serving former defence minister and former Robben Island prisoner dies
all.description: Mosiuoa Lekota was imprisoned with former South African President Nelson Mandela on Robben Island.

all.headline: Inside the Trump administration’s scramble to support its own war
all.description: One State Department official said too few people were read in on war plans.

all.headline: Iran war challenges Cyprus’ time in the EU hot seat
all.description: The tiny island nation finds itself under fire just after taking over the reins of the EU for a six-month stint.

all.headline: Britain looks to Frederiksen’s migration plan to neutralize Farage
all.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is eager to take on complaints from liberal-minded colleagues — and combat Nigel Farage's right-wing insurgency.

all.headline: Trump’s lightbulb moment: America needs Europe after all
all.description: The U.S. is even looking to Ukraine to help its operations against Iran — as Trump rages at Starmer and Sánchez for refusing to back his war.

all.headline: MAGA embrace: Europe’s nationalists head to Washington
Trump’s Iran gamble carries a political cost
all.description: The decision to go to war was a dangerous wager that force could achieve what diplomacy couldn't — and it'll likely cause the president trouble come November.
